title: Arabic Has Only Three Word Types

ayah.meaning: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful

hook.question: You've said this thousands of times. It has 4 words. Can you tell what grammatical role each word plays? By the end of World 1 — you will.

concept.title: The Three Families: Ism, Fi'l, Harf
concept.body: Every single word in the Arabic language — and therefore every word in the Quran — belongs to exactly one of three families. Not four. Not ten. Three. This is the entire map of Arabic.

families.name: Ism
families.description: A person, place, thing, quality, or idea. About 50% of the Quran is Ism.
families.quickTest: Can you put 'the' in front of it? → It's probably an Ism. 'The book', 'the mercy', 'the Lord'

families.name: Fi'l
families.description: An action or occurrence. Carries time — past, present, or future. About 30% of the Quran.
families.quickTest: Does it describe something happening? → It's probably a Fi'l. 'He said', 'they believe', 'it was created'

families.name: Harf
families.description: Everything else. Prepositions, conjunctions, question words, negation. No meaning alone — only with other words.
families.quickTest: Not a noun, not a verb? → It's a Harf. 'In', 'and', 'from', 'not', 'indeed'

examples.reason: A name. Names are always Ism.
An action in the past. He created.
A preposition meaning 'in'. Not a noun or verb.
A quality — mercy. You can say 'the mercy' → Ism.
Connector meaning 'and'. No standalone meaning → Harf.

breakdown.note: بِ = particle (in/by) + اسم = name (Ism). Together: 'in the name of'
The name of God. Always Ism.
The Most Merciful — a quality/name of Allah. Ism.
The Ever Merciful — another quality/name. Ism.

unlock.insight: Bismillah is almost entirely Ism. Three names of Allah. You've been beginning with the names of God — now you know it grammatically.

quiz.question: How many word types exist in Arabic?
quiz.explanation: Exactly three: Ism (noun), Fi'l (verb), Harf (particle). Every word in the Quran is one of these.

quiz.question: What family does اللَّه (Allah) belong to?
quiz.explanation: Allah is a name — and names are always Ism.

quiz.question: What family does وَ (and) belong to?
quiz.explanation: Connectors like 'and' are Harf — they connect other words and have no standalone meaning.

quiz.question: Roughly what percentage of the Quran is made of Ism?
quiz.explanation: About 50% of Quranic words are Ism. This is why World 2 — the world of Ism — is the most important world in this course.
